data.narrative:
LuxWall is a Series B-stage climate and energy company that has raised $84M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of six same-sector, same-stage private companies—including Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M)—plus one public comparable.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $84M total raised as a proxy for entry valuation and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $160M. Peer-median analysis across the six comparable private companies yields a median valuation of $355M, with a range spanning $120M to $5.0B. The dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and capital intensity within the climate-tech sector.

Triangulating both methods produces a valuation range of $136M (low), $257M (median), and $408M (high). The wide spread between methods—$160M versus $355M—reflects material uncertainty in where LuxWall sits within the peer distribution. Key sensitivities include technology readiness relative to more mature peers and competitive positioning in what remains a crowded frontier-energy market. Additional transparency on revenue traction and customer commitments would narrow this band.
